steps.title: Recognize the parallel lines condition
steps.content: A system has no solution when the two equations represent parallel lines — lines with the same slope but different y-intercepts. When parallel lines are part of a system, they never intersect, leaving no point that satisfies both equations.

steps.title: Rewrite the first equation in standard form and check choice A
steps.content:
Rewrite the given equation $x - 8y = 12$ in a form that reveals parallel options. Multiply choice A by 2:
$$\frac{1}{2}x - 4y = 0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x - 8y = 0$$
Now compare:
- First equation: $x - 8y = 12$
- Choice A (scaled): $x - 8y = 0$

Both have identical coefficients on the left ($x - 8y$), but different constants on the right ($12 \neq 0$). This means the lines are parallel.

steps.title: Verify by solving the system
steps.content:
Attempt to solve the system:
$$x - 8y = 12$$$$\frac{1}{2}x - 4y = 0$$
From the second equation: $\frac{1}{2}x = 4y \Rightarrow x = 8y$

Substitute into the first equation:
$$8y - 8y = 12$$$$0 = 12$$
This is a contradiction — no values of $x$ and $y$ can satisfy both equations simultaneously. That matches choice A — A is correct.
